symbols: cruciform arrangement — the cross of Christ abstracted into geometric form
description: An abstract geometric composition on a pale gray-lavender background. Four identical purple four-pointed star shapes are arranged in a cross formation at center, connected and surrounded by yellow biomorphic jack-like forms that radiate outward symmetrically. The design is flat, clean, and centrally symmetrical.
theological_description: The title 'Crucifixion' invites a reading of this abstract geometric pattern as a meditation on the cross. The cruciform arrangement of the four central shapes preserves the essential geometry of the cross while stripping away all figurative content. This abstraction raises the question of whether the cross can retain its theological power when reduced to pure form. The symmetry and order of the composition may suggest the cosmic order restored through Christ's sacrifice — the cross as the axis mundi around which all creation is reoriented. The purple color traditionally associated with Lent and Passion, combined with the gold of resurrection glory, maintains a liturgical resonance even in abstract form.

scripture_refs.reason: The word of the cross — the crucifixion reduced to its essential sign and meaning
Boasting only in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ — the cross as the central Christian symbol
